title: Requesting an appointment — overview
content: Mayo Clinic offers three intake paths: new patients submit information online and choose a follow-up time; returning patients request through their patient account; referring physicians have a separate path. Appointments can also be requested by phone during local business hours. The online form lets patients request a callback rather than wait on hold. Emergencies should be directed to 911 rather than the appointment line.

title: U.S. appointment phone numbers and hours
content: Mayo Clinic in Minnesota (Rochester) Central Appointment Office: 507-538-3270, 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. Central time, Monday-Friday. Minnesota Children's: 855-629-6543, same hours. Mayo Clinic in Arizona (Phoenix/Scottsdale) Central Appointment Office: 480-301-8484, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Mountain time, Monday-Friday. Mayo Clinic in Florida (Jacksonville) Central Appointment Office: 904-953-0853, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Eastern time, Monday-Friday. Mayo Clinic Health System locations (Iowa, Minnesota, Wisconsin) are listed at mayoclinichealthsystem.org/request-appointment/phone.

title: How the appointment request process works
content: An appointment coordinator is assigned to build a care team and issue a full visit itinerary. A specific provider cannot be guaranteed. Wait times vary by medical need and specialty. After an itinerary is issued, the coordinating provider reviews medical history, orders necessary tests, and presents results and recommendations at the end of the visit.

title: Referrals
content: Mayo Clinic generally does not require a doctor's referral. However, the patient's insurance plan may require one. Patients should check with their insurance company; if a referral is needed, Mayo can help. Physicians who refer patients have a separate intake path.

title: Conditions and specialties treated
content: Mayo Clinic is consistently ranked among the top U.S. hospitals and is regularly recognized in Cancer, Cardiology and Heart Surgery, Diabetes and Endocrinology, Gastroenterology, Geriatrics, Gynecology, Neurology and Neurosurgery, Orthopedics, Pulmonology, and Urology.

title: Mayo Clinic locations
content: Three U.S. campuses: Mayo Clinic in Arizona (Phoenix/Scottsdale), Mayo Clinic in Florida (Jacksonville), Mayo Clinic in Minnesota (Rochester). The Mayo Clinic Health System is a regional network serving communities in southern Minnesota, western Wisconsin, and northern Iowa.

title: Patient Travel Services
content: Mayo Clinic offers Patient Travel Services to help patients plan their trip — hotel accommodations, transportation, and special accommodations based on health needs. Packing checklists and campus maps are available in advance.

title: Billing and insurance overview
content: Mayo Clinic's billing area covers: paying bills online, preparing before arrival, accepted insurance plans, insurance and self-pay options, understanding bills, insurer approvals, price estimates and per-campus transparency, charitable care, copay assistance, and the federal No Surprises Act. Patient Account Services handles questions.

title: Before arriving — what to address
content: Before a Mayo Clinic visit, patients should: complete the patient and financial checklist; obtain insurance pre-authorizations; sign the Mayo Clinic Authorizations and Service Terms form (required for all patients or guardians prior to care); complete registration; and arrange any required pre-service deposits.

title: Insurance types accepted
content: Mayo Clinic has arrangements with several types of insurers: contracted plans, Medicare, Medicaid, TRICARE, HMOs, out-of-network noncontracted plans, and other types like disability, motor vehicle, prescription card, and workers' compensation. Patients should verify their specific plan with the campus they plan to visit.

title: Insurance approvals — pre-certification and prior authorizations
content: Many plans require pre-certification before care. Investigating coverage can take up to six weeks. Services that may require pre-certification: outpatient and inpatient hospital services, observation, invasive procedures, CT, MRI, PET scans, colonoscopies. Patients are responsible for knowing plan requirements. If prior authorization isn't obtained, the patient may owe more — possibly all — of the cost. A pre-service deposit may be required.

title: Uninsured (self-pay) or underinsured patients
content: "Underinsured" can mean a high deductible, high out-of-pocket maximum, limited-benefit plan, or out-of-network plan. With out-of-network insurance, patients are responsible for the difference between Mayo Clinic's rates and what insurance pays. Mayo Clinic offers an Uninsured Discount Policy: discounted prices on medically necessary care for qualified uninsured U.S. residents (not applied to cosmetic or non-medically-necessary elective care). Eligibility requires applying for Medicaid or other available assistance first.

title: Pre-service deposits
content: Pre-service deposits are required for: elective cosmetic surgery, noncontracted (out-of-network or reference-based payer) plans, plans requiring referral, health ministry reimbursement plans, and services denied by insurance. Amounts are based on estimates. Refunds are issued if deposits exceed actual charges. Financial Counseling can prepare estimates — call 1-507-284-2421 or 1-833-479-5483.

title: Charitable care and financial assistance
content: Mayo Clinic offers financial assistance for patients unable to pay for medically necessary care. Financial assistance documents are published separately for Arizona, Florida, and Minnesota campuses. A Copay and Grant Assistance program helps with out-of-pocket drug costs.

title: Price estimates and transparency
content: Mayo Clinic offers an online Cost Estimator at estimator.mayoclinic.org for estimating service costs. Each campus also publishes price transparency pages (Arizona, Florida, Minnesota). CMS chargemaster pricing including COVID-19 test pricing is available. Personalized estimates reflecting a patient's insurance can be requested in advance.

title: No Surprises Act
content: Mayo Clinic complies with the federal No Surprises Act, which provides consumer protections against surprise medical billing.

title: Billing and insurance contact
content: Billing and insurance questions go to Mayo Clinic Patient Account Services. A glossary of billing and insurance terms, topic-based FAQs, and a page on understanding the Mayo Clinic bill are also available.

title: Patient Online Services and the Mayo Clinic app
content: Patient Online Services is Mayo Clinic's secure portal. Existing patients view test results, message their care team, request or view appointments, pay bills, and access medical records. Available on the web and through the Mayo Clinic app (iOS and Android). New patients typically create an account after their first appointment is scheduled.
